description: §14.8.9 egress redaction — the RUNTIME half of item 2/5 (ss60-parked, adapter-gated). A client button calls the server fn getUser (a SELECT * over a protect= table); the E-ADAPTER server-eval path runs the REAL emitted route handler, so _scrml_protect_redact executes at the egress sink and the client-bound cell @user observes the payload with the protected column ABSENT ({id, name} — no passwordHash). This is the client-visible absence the fetch-mock could never show (the mock returned the serverStub verbatim, bypassing the sink). Compile half stays pinned: I-PROTECT-STRIP-001 (info) names the stripped column.
rationale: §14.8.9 (SPEC.md I-PROTECT-STRIP-001 row): 'The compiler-emitted egress serializer stripped one or more protected-origin columns from a client-egress payload — a server-function return ... before it crossed to the client.' The runtime proof is that the client cell holds {id, name} only; passwordHash never crosses. auth="none" declassifies the AUTH scope (dodging the auth-path CSRF gate) while the §14.8.9 column-redaction floor stays engaged (column-redaction ⟂ auth-scope, SPEC.md §52 line ~30500).
